title: Inauguration talk, breaking down the $1.9T stimulus, the case for recalling Gavin Newsom & more

Show Notes:

0:00 Bestie intro, Inauguration talk, Impeachment implications

20:22 Trump as a successful change agent, Facebook's Oversight Board overseeing Trump's appeal

34:07 Will Big Tech be regulated like utilities? Friedberg tells a spy story

44:15 Breaking down the $1.9T Stimulus package, needs for infrastructure bill, worst-case scenarios

55:06 How the tech ecosystem plays into inequality & how to fix it

1:04:00 Recalling California Governor Gavin Newsom, California's lockdown incompetence

1:17:32 Sacks rebukes cancel culture, this time from the right-wing mob

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses the inauguration, breaks down the $1.9T stimulus package, and debates the case for recalling Gavin Newsom. They also touch on Big Tech regulation, inequality, and cancel culture.

takeaways.text: The $1.9T stimulus package may have long-term implications for the economy
Big Tech may face increased regulation
Inequality is a growing concern in the tech ecosystem
Cancel culture is a threat to free speech
The case for recalling Gavin Newsom is based on lockdown incompetence

sentiment.notes: The hosts express concern about the long-term implications of the stimulus package and the growing threat of cancel culture.

notable_moments.description: Discussion of Trump as a successful change agent
